definition: average ratio of the duration of energization to the total period in which intermittent or continuous or temporary duty takes place

definition_ml.en: average ratio of the duration of energization to the total period in which intermittent or continuous or temporary duty takes place

note:
NOTE 1: The duty factor may be expressed as a percentage of the total period. 
NOTE 2: The factor varies between 0 and 100 %; 100 % indicates that the component  is continuously in operation; 50 % indicates that the component is, within a given period, half of that period intermitting in operation.
NOTE 3: The value of the factor  is normally specified by the user in charge of the process.

note_ml.en:
NOTE 1: The duty factor may be expressed as a percentage of the total period. 
NOTE 2: The factor varies between 0 and 100 %; 100 % indicates that the component  is continuously in operation; 50 % indicates that the component is, within a given period, half of that period intermitting in operation.
NOTE 3: The value of the factor  is normally specified by the user in charge of the process.
